summary: Session 3 of 5: the failure matrix and the honest failure UX (critique C3). New pure composer describeSeatSetupOutcome(outcome, providerKeysPresent, rerunHint) in copilotSeatSetup.ts, keyed on the SAME DABBLER_* presence probe the Full-tier inline key warning uses (providerKeyPresent) - every failure branch (insufficient-providers, refresh-failed, cancelled, config-write-failed, and gitScaffold's skip-install-incomplete) lands in exactly one of two honest states: keyless => 'router is not yet functional' + reason-specific fix guidance; keys present => api affirmed as a genuinely working fallback. insufficient-providers distinguishes confirmed==0 (CLI missing / not signed in - re-run can help) from a 1-provider enterprise-locked seat (re-run will not change the result); config-write-failed instructs the one-field hand edit, no re-probe. Both named S2 residuals closed: (1) atomic config write - writeConfigAtomically stages <config>.dabbler-seat-setup.tmp then renames (optional FileOps.rename, real ops use fs.renameSync; no-rename fallback keeps writing; failed rename cleans temp and surfaces as config-write-failed; guarantee deliberately scoped to process-crash atomic replacement, not power-loss durability); (2) POSIX process-tree kill - the refresh child spawns detached on POSIX (own group leader) and cancel dispatches through the extracted, exported dispatchKill(platform, pid, KillEffects): win32 taskkill /T (async spawn-error fallback via makeRealKillEffects), POSIX process.kill(-pid, SIGTERM), sync-throw fallback to plain kill. requiresE2E 'suggested' resolved: NO new Playwright Layer-3 spec - the async-progress surface is a native vscode notification the webview harness cannot drive; all logic behind it is Layer-2-pinned; decision + reasoning recorded (s3.e2e-decision). 31 new Layer-2 tests (1206 -> 1237 passing). Real induced-failure dogfood: missing-binary run => insufficient-providers with the keyless honest message in 0.3s; REAL mid-run cancel (copilot CLI 1.0.68, 15s in) killed the python child AND both live copilot descendants (taskkill /T verified), restored the lockfile to pre-run absent; real-NTFS temp+rename replaced an existing config with no tmp left.
verification_notes: Routed gate REQUIRED (blast-radius, multi-module, breadth). Routed code-review first (opus -> gpt-5-4 auto-verify VERIFIED): 1 Major (POSIX kill dispatch had zero unit coverage -> extracted injectable dispatchKill + 5 tests) + 2 Minors + 3 suggestions, all fixed in-session. Session verification on gpt-5-4 (OpenAI, cross-provider from the Claude orchestrator): R1 returned two findings with no verdict token and no severities - treated as blocking per the L-071-1 anti-laundering rule; ledger ids S3-V-001 (async taskkill-error fallback untested -> makeRealKillEffects factory with TaskkillSpawn seam + 3 pinning tests) and S3-V-002 (skip-install honesty tests missing the negative half -> fixed). R2 (narrow, fixes-only) VERIFIED with both ledger ids resolved and no regression. Known, honestly-scoped limitations carried to S5 docs: the POSIX group-kill is unit-pinned but not live-dogfooded (win32 host); atomicity is process-crash-scoped (no fsync).

reason.specifics: Session 4 is Feature 2 (simplified verification-mode copy): a small copy decision plus a repo-wide stale-quote sweep - mechanical breadth, not deep reasoning. The routed ai-assignment recommendation (authored at set start) is claude-fable-5 @ medium with routed code-review as the completeness net; nothing in Sessions 1-3 changed that calculus, and Session 4 is deliberately independent of Feature 1 (critique m6).
